core_model.structure_summary: A fixed-point iteration that repeatedly takes a damped forward step against a maximal monotone operator formed by interconnecting component operators, converging to the root where the operator vanishes.
core_model.canonical_form: x^{j+1} = x^{j} - \alpha\, S(x^{j}), \qquad S = S_1 + S_2, \quad \alpha = m/L^2; \qquad \text{fixed point: } 0 \in S(x^\star)

variables.meaning: an iterated trajectory (periodic signal) in a function space being driven to a fixed point
a step size set by monotonicity/Lipschitz constants and the interconnection structure of component operators
the residual of a maximal monotone operator whose zero is sought

deterministic_skeleton.summary: The continuous-time limit of the damped iteration is a gradient-flow-type descent along a maximal monotone operator, relaxing to the operator's zero.

notes: Central contribution is an operator-splitting fixed-point iteration for the periodic (steady) behavior of interconnected maximal monotone systems; taken as core as a discrete update, with the underlying monotone inclusion 0 in S(x). Skeleton is the continuous-time descent flow whose equilibrium solves the inclusion. Monotonicity plays the structural role that convexity plays for gradient descent.
